title: Optimize iOS device interaction latency and Thin Harness follow-ups
summary: Continue from the merged iOS Fast Path V1. Reduce end-to-end iOS interaction latency using measured session reuse, tiered evidence and repeatable benchmarks, then close remaining evidence-backed Thin Harness follow-ups.
goals: Implement a bounded short-session execution path that reduces scheduler and process startup overhead.
Use settle results and exact waits before scoped or full accessibility snapshots.
Measure cold and warm simulator and optional physical-device latency by segment.
Reduce remaining measured Thin Harness fixed costs without increasing the stable MCP tool surface.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-device and per-session serialization, timeout, cancellation and redaction remain enforced.
Exact-result iOS workflows avoid full accessibility-tree capture.
Benchmarks report p50 and p95 for cold and warm runs without fabricated baselines.
Eligible Thin Harness Fast operations still create zero ExecutionJob, LocalJob and Worker.
Focused tests, type, runtime architecture and MCP compatibility checks pass.

tasks.title: Implement bounded iOS session direct execution
tasks.objective: Design and implement a short allowlisted interaction-session execution path with ownership, cancellation, timeout, receipt and durable escalation.
acceptanceCriteria: No concurrent writes to one device or interaction.
Short session actions avoid one durable scheduler job per action.
Long or recoverable work still escalates to Durable execution.

tasks.title: Complete tiered iOS accessibility evidence
tasks.objective: Add selector or ref reuse, settle diff, exact wait, scoped snapshot and full snapshot fallback tiers with bounded redacted evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Snapshot cost is measured by evidence tier.

tasks.title: Build repeatable iOS latency benchmark
tasks.objective: Measure controller overhead, process startup, runner round trips, settle, snapshot and artifact costs for cold and warm simulator and optional physical-device runs.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one warmup and three measured runs per case.
Unavailable historical baselines are not synthesized.

tasks.title: Close measured Thin Harness follow-ups
tasks.objective: Measure and reduce patch savepoint and command-policy costs, large-repository read variance, Workbench schema size and Fast/Durable adapter duplication.
acceptanceCriteria: Fast eligible operations retain zero durable side effects.
